name: Week 10 Push — Form-First Return (Spotlight: Push Press + Incline DB)
description: Form-first cycle, NOT a return-to-PR week. Weights pulled back ~10-15% from W8. The progression this week is the form. **Spotlight lifts (drill these all session):** push press and incline DB press. Everything else holds and gets sharp cues only. Goal: leave the gym feeling like every rep was honest.

dayOverview.theme: Form-first return from W9 deload. Modern 2026 cues, lighter loads, cleaner reps.

spotlights.why: W7 found parallel feet > duck feet. W10 locks that in plus rib-down + vertical bar path. Make it automatic this week.
W9D1 discovered the forearm-stacked-over-elbow cue makes 40lb honest. Drill that cue until it's the default setup, not a thought.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W10 push press + incline DB · W11 chest press machine · W12 lateral raise + cable fly · W13 cable lat raise + tri ext + straight arm pulldown. ~4 weeks per cycle, then refresh.
watchpoints: Right scap → front-pec referral on chest press (W9D1) — slow tempo first, drop weight only if needed
Yuri band warmup is non-negotiable for shoulder tolerance
If a cue can't be held, the cue wins — drop the weight or the rep

exercises.notes: Slow, big circles. Wake up scap glide before loading.
Full sequence with red band. The shoulder won't tolerate cable work without it — never skip on push day.

Sets the press template for the day.

**Form cues:**
- Ribs down, glutes tight, body in one line — no pike, no sag
- Hands stack DIRECTLY under shoulders (not in front, not wide)
- Slow eccentric, 1s pause with chest 1-2" off the floor

**LOAD:** 40/side (one notch back from W8's 45 — give the form room to rebuild).

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every set:**
- **Feet parallel, hip-width** — NOT duck stance. The W7 discovery, locked in.
- **Ribs DOWN before the dip** — squeeze the front of your ribs toward your hips. No rib flare = no lower-back compensation.
- **Shallow dip** (~3-4 inches), straight down through the heels. The dip is for legs to drive, not for momentum.
- **Vertical DB path** — dumbbells go from front-of-shoulders straight up to *over the heels* at lockout (two parallel lines next to your ears). Head pokes through at the top. If the DBs drift forward, the lockout is fake.
- **Breath:** in at the dip, out on the drive.

**Reflection:** which cue slipped first when you got tired? That's next week's drill. Stop the set if rib position breaks.

**LOAD:** 20lb (hold). Sharp cues only — this is a hold week for laterals.
- Soft elbow ~10-15° bend (locked elbow stresses the joint, recruits traps)
- Lead with the elbow, hand passive
- Stop at shoulder height — higher just shrugs traps
- 2-second eccentric

**LOAD:** 165 (back off from W8's 180/185). Hold-and-clean week — spotlight is W11.
- Seat aligned so handles hit **mid-chest / nipple line**, NOT shoulder height
- Set scaps back AND down into the pad before unracking
- Slow eccentric, 3 seconds down
- Press to ALMOST lockout — leave a hair of bend
- **Watch:** right scap → front-pec referral (W9D1). If it twinges, slow down further before dropping weight.

**LOAD:** 45lb pair (back off from W8's 50).

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every set. The W9D1 discovery is the foundation:**
- **Bench at ~30°**, NOT 45°. Steeper = more front delt, less upper chest.
- **Shoulders packed back AND down** — pin the back of the shoulder into the bench, like you're trying to break it in half with your scaps.
- **Forearm stacked vertically over the elbow at the bottom** — elbow directly under the wrist when DBs are at chest level. *This* is what made 40lb feel hard W9D1. The cue IS the progression.
- **Elbows ~45° from torso** — not flared 90°, not tucked tight
- **Pause 1s at the bottom** — "dead weight" before driving up
- Press to a fingerwidth apart at the top. No DB clack.

**Swap option:** if the shoulder feels off, swap to seated DB shoulder press at 35-40lb using the W9D1 rotation cue — DBs facing IN at the bottom (palms toward face), rotating to facing-OUT at the top. That cue gives the shoulder real relief.

**Reflection:** does the forearm stack feel automatic by set 4, or are you still thinking about it?

**LOAD:** Kinesis 8 (hold).
- Slight forward hinge at hips — chest leads, not head
- Soft elbow ~10-15°, fixed throughout
- Think "hug a tree" — squeeze from chest, not arms
- Stop a fistwidth apart at top, no crash
- Slow eccentric for full pec stretch

**LOAD:** cable column ~30-40 (W7-W8 working range was 45-50; pull back ~10-15% for form-first). One arm at a time, 3 sets per side.
- Stand sideways to the machine, working arm farther from the stack
- Soft elbow ~10°, fixed throughout
- Lead with elbow, hand passive — wrist follows
- Stop at shoulder height (any higher = traps)
- Fight the cable on the way down, no snap-back
- **W10 trick that worked:** massage out right pec between sets — kills the crackle. Roll/engage scapula too.

**LOAD:** ~50 (cable column kneeling — your actual setup, not 'incline' as the name implies).
- Kneel facing away from the stack, rope attachment overhead
- Torso slightly forward, head between arms — that's your 'incline' position
- Elbows pinned, only forearms move
- Full stretch at the bottom, long head loads deep
- Stop just shy of lockout at top, slow eccentric

**LOAD:** cable column ~100 (W7=110, W8=120; W10 form-first pullback). Cable column has been the setup the entire program — W7-W8 logs and W10D1 actuals all confirm.
- Stand a step back, hinge ~15° at hips
- Slight elbow bend (5°), fixed throughout — perfectly straight = injury risk
- Pull to **thighs**, NOT past — past = shoulders roll forward, lats off
- Squeeze lats hard at bottom — "oranges into back pockets"
- Slow controlled return to full overhead stretch

tips: **FORM-FIRST WEEK** — every weight is intentionally below W8. The progression IS the form, not the load.
**Spotlight lifts** are push press + incline DB — drill those cues every set. Other lifts get sharp/short cues only.
**Rotation plan:** W11 chest press · W12 lateral raise + cable fly · W13 cable lat raise + tri ext + straight arm pulldown. ~4-week cycle.
If a cue can't be held, the cue wins — drop weight, slow tempo, or skip the rep. Don't grind a bad rep.
Yuri band warmup is non-negotiable. Push press swap to seated DB shoulder press (rotation cue) is a legit option if the shoulder isn't ready to stand-and-load.
Rest 2 min push press, 2.5 min chest press (form takes more rest than ego does), 75s cables.
formPhilosophy: Form-first base-building. Modern 2026 cues, not 2010 bro-science. Joint stacking, ROM, tempo, breath are the progressions. Load is the last lever. Building decades, not weeks.
